pi_items.source: Confirmed via textile industry standards and LCA literature
Wikipedia: Units of textile measurement; eFunda: Linear Mass Density
Confirmed via LCA studies: ACS Sustainable Chemistry & Engineering article on recycled polyester staple fibers
Wikipedia: Units of textile measurement; eFunda confirms tex = 1 gram per 1000 meters
A&E Gütermann and textile conversion sources confirm: 1 tex = 10 dtex, therefore 1 dtex = 0.1 tex
Multiple sources confirm: denier = grams per 9000m, tex = grams per 1000m; therefore 1 denier = 1/9 tex ≈ 0.111111
No specific textile unit conversion tolerance standard found; 0.01 is a reasonable floating-point comparison tolerance

privileged_info_summary: The task involves creating textile units (tex, dtex, denier) for measuring linear density in openLCA. All unit conversion factors are verified as scientifically accurate: tex is the reference unit (1.0), dtex equals 0.1 tex (1 tex = 10 dtex), and denier equals 0.111111 tex (1 tex = 9 denier). Polyester staple fiber is a real material commonly used in textile and LCA studies. The unit group name and flow property name are appropriately labeled for linear density measurement.
